criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose anterior myocardial infarction, which of the following diagnostic criterion should be evaluated?
options: Presence of inverted T waves in lateral leads
Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in leads V2, V3, and V4
Presence of scooped ST-segment depression in precordial leads
Presence of diffuse concave ST-segment elevation across precordial leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST elevation in at least two of the anterior leads, including leads V1, V2, V3, and V4?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show ST elevation? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complexes with ST elevation? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have anterior myocardial infarction?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
